title: Frontend Implementation for Realtime Visitor Statistics System

requirements.summary: Implement automatic visitor tracking middleware that executes on every public page load, capturing session data and sending it to the backend
acceptanceCriteria: Tracking logic executes automatically on public page renders
session_id persisted in sessionStorage and reused across pages
device_type and browser detected from navigator.userAgent
Bot user agents filtered out before calling backend

file_operations.description: Create a custom React hook that implements automatic visitor tracking. The hook should: generate or retrieve session_id from sessionStorage, detect device_type and browser from navigator.userAgent, capture current page URL and referrer from window.location, exclude admin routes (/admin/*) and known bot user agents, call backend trackVisitor() method with all collected data on mount. Use useEffect with pathname dependency to trigger on navigation.

Import and invoke useVisitorTracking hook at the top level of the PublicLayout component to ensure automatic tracking executes on every public page render. This wires the tracking middleware into all public routes without requiring individual page modifications.

Create utility functions for visitor tracking: generateSessionId() to create unique session identifiers, getOrCreateSessionId() to manage sessionStorage persistence, detectDeviceType() to parse user agent for device classification, detectBrowser() to identify browser from user agent, isBot() to filter known bot user agents. Export these utilities for use by the tracking hook.

requirements.summary: Update VisitorStatsPage to fetch and display realtime statistics with auto-refresh every 15 seconds
acceptanceCriteria: VisitorStatsPage displays all 8 statistic values from backend
Statistics auto-refresh every 15 seconds via React Query

file_operations.description: Update the VisitorStatsPage component to display all 8 statistics fields from the backend VisitorStats type: totalVisitors, todayVisitors, yesterdayVisitors, weeklyVisitors, monthlyVisitors, yearlyVisitors, onlineUsers, and pageViews (mapped from totalPageViews). Use the useVisitorStats hook with refetchInterval set to 15000ms for automatic refresh. Remove any hardcoded zero values and ensure all data comes from the backend response. Add loading skeleton states during data fetch.

requirements.summary: Add line chart showing 30-day visitor trend and bar chart showing top 10 most visited pages to VisitorStatsPage
acceptanceCriteria: Line chart renders 30-day visitor trend data
Bar chart renders top 10 pages by view count

file_operations.description: Create a React Query hook that calls backend getVisitorTrendLast30Days() method to fetch daily visitor counts for the last 30 days. Return the data as an array of tuples [timestamp, count]. Include error handling and loading states. Set refetchInterval to 15000ms to match the stats refresh rate.

Create a React Query hook that calls backend getPageViewsByUrl() method to fetch page view counts grouped by URL. Return the data as an array of tuples [url, count]. Include error handling and loading states. Set refetchInterval to 15000ms to match the stats refresh rate.

Create a line chart component that visualizes 30-day visitor trend data. Use a charting library (recharts recommended for React) to render the chart. Accept trend data as props in the format Array<[timestamp, count]>. Convert timestamps to readable dates for x-axis labels. Handle empty data gracefully with a placeholder message. Include loading state support.

Create a bar chart component that visualizes top 10 most visited pages. Use a charting library (recharts recommended) to render horizontal bars. Accept page view data as props in the format Array<[url, count]>. Slice to top 10 entries. Truncate long URLs for readability. Handle empty data gracefully with a placeholder message. Include loading state support.

Import and integrate VisitorTrendChart and TopPagesChart components into the VisitorStatsPage layout. Use useVisitorTrend and usePageViews hooks to fetch data for the charts. Position charts below the statistics cards in a responsive grid layout. Pass fetched data and loading states to chart components.

requirements.summary: Update useVisitorStats hook to properly call backend getVisitorStats method without stubbed logic
acceptanceCriteria: useVisitorStats hook calls backend getVisitorStats method
Hook returns actual backend data without modification
Error handling implemented for failed requests

file_operations.description: Review and update the useVisitorStats hook to ensure it properly calls the backend actor's getVisitorStats() method and returns the actual VisitorStats data structure. Remove any placeholder or stubbed logic. Verify error handling for network failures and auth issues. The backend method already exists and returns the correct data type, so focus on ensuring the frontend hook correctly invokes it and processes the response.
